name: Carabidae
diagnosis: A large, cosmopolitan family of predominantly predaceous beetles. Body usually elongate-oval and depressed; head prognathous with sharp mandibles. Antennae filiform, eleven-segmented, inserted laterally between mandible and eye. Pronotum typically with distinct lateral margins; elytra usually striate, frequently with punctate striae. Tarsi 5-5-5; males of many genera with dilated proxtarsi. Species are most diverse in mesic woodlands, riparian corridors, and disturbed agricultural margins.

genus_notes.calosoma: Large, brilliantly colored ground beetles known as caterpillar hunters. Active climbers and predators of lepidopteran larvae.
genus_notes.carabus: Robust, often metallic ground beetles with striate, costate elytra. Largely flightless; most species inhabit deciduous leaf litter.
genus_notes.scaphinotus: Snail-eating ground beetles. Head and prothorax elongate-narrow, an adaptation for entering gastropod shells.
genus_notes.pasimachus: Large, deep-black, flightless ground beetles with sickle-like mandibles. Open woodlands and tallgrass remnants.
genus_notes.harpalus: Speciose genus of seed-feeding and omnivorous ground beetles; common in disturbed and agricultural habitats.
genus_notes.scarites: Scarites is a genus of ground beetle native to the Palearctic, the Near East, North America and North Africa.
genus_notes.stenolophus: Stenolophus is a genus of ground beetle native to the Palearctic (including Europe), the Nearctic, the Near East, and North Africa.
